definitions.definition: Describing a system or behavior in which an AI model makes its own decisions about what actions to take, including whether to call tools.
definitions.example: An agentic assistant decides to call a dictionary tool when it needs a definition.

definitions.definition: The process of giving an LLM access to information it could not infer from training alone, such as a live API response or a curated document.
definitions.example: Providing a tool result to the model is one form of grounding.

definitions.definition: The practice of designing and refining prompts so that an LLM performs a task more reliably and accurately.
definitions.example: I used prompt engineering to teach the model when to call the lookup tool.

definitions.definition: In MCP, a tool is a function or API the model can call to retrieve information or perform an action that it cannot do from training alone.
definitions.example: The model called the lookup_dictionary_entry tool to get a definition.

definitions.definition: A set of criteria used to evaluate a project or assignment.
definitions.example: I used the Project 3 rubric to design the agentic behavior and documentation.
